halt_reason_1: A sector script depends on library(xlsx), which needs a Java runtime that is not available in the reviewer environment.
halt_reason_2: A sector script expects a different Excel column schema than the canonical shared Excel file provides — likely the wrong sheet/file, or a producer working copy that differs from the deposited one.
halt_reason_3: A WT classification script requires a cached upstream population artifact that exists only on the producer side, not in shared storage.
fixes_attempted: Installed the required R packages (the Java-dependent Excel package still fails on Java load)
Ported the sector scripts from the Java-dependent Excel package to openxlsx (pure-R, no Java)
Added a read-shim so the openxlsx reader matches the prior call signature
Removed an environment-clearing call that wiped driver-set globals
Set the required path globals (dwCodes and the shared-storage path globals) explicitly
blockers_for_dbm: Three DBM-actionable items: (1) Java dependency — a sector script needs library(xlsx); the sector lead should port it to openxlsx (pure-R, no Java) so the pipeline runs without a Java runtime; (2) an Excel column-schema mismatch — sector code expects a different column schema than the canonical shared Excel file provides, so the sector lead must reconcile; (3) an undeposited upstream population cache — a WT script requires a cached population artifact that exists only on the producer side, so it must be deposited to shared storage or its regeneration documented.
